title: research case-bank entry
description: One specific, verbatim, ground-truth case — NOT a summary. Summaries smooth over the particular example an LLM needs to reason from; this store preserves the raw case + provenance + the generative payload (what it seeds). Core fields are genre-independent; `extension` carries genre-specific structure. Append-only jsonl, one entry per line. Follows substrate/schemas SchemaVer + content-addressed-identity conventions.

schema_version.description: SchemaVer (MODEL-REVISION-ADDITION), NOT SemVer. New genres = ADDITION; changing a required field = MODEL.

conformsTo.description: Per-record schema URI. Readers route by this first, fall back to schema_version.

case_id.description: cb_ + first 16 hex of sha256_hex(canonical_json({domain, genre, verbatim, provenance.source})). Content-addressed → idempotent append; the same case re-added collapses to one row.

domain.description: Topic/area the case belongs to: a research topic prefix (immigration-fiscal, iq-sex-differences) OR a methodology area (agent-review, eval-design, manim-expression).

genre.description: What kind of case. ground-truth-exemplar: a specific real example to reason from (the primary research use — the thing summaries lose). defect-trap: a verified defect for detection/eval gold. capability-target: a target pattern from an external gold corpus. failure-mode: a way a system broke. generator-seed: a case that minted an idea-generator. Add a genre = ADDITION bump.

verbatim.description: THE load-bearing field. The specific case text, PRESERVED VERBATIM — the actual excerpt/quote/artifact, not a paraphrase. May be markdown. minLength is the anti-summary guard: a one-line gist is not a case. If the real artifact is large, store the decisive passage verbatim and point to the full source via provenance.source_ref.

payload.description: The generative content — what this case SEEDS: the lesson, the trap, the capability, the pattern. This is why it's a bank and not an archive. Distinct from verbatim: verbatim is the raw case; payload is what to DO with it.

source.description: Where the verbatim came from: a URL, a citation, or a repo path + commit (e.g. evals/critique_replay/cases.md C5, or doi:..., or https://...).

source_ref.description: Optional pointer to the FULL source when verbatim is an excerpt: corpus source_id (cases may cite the corpus; they are not corpus documents), file path, or anchor.

tag.description: Canonical provenance tag — the bare token of skills/references/provenance-tags.md (SOURCE | DATABASE | DATA | INFERENCE | SPEC | CALC | QUOTE | TRAINING-DATA | PREPRINT | FRONTIER | UNVERIFIED) or a source-grade A1..F6. Validated by loading hooks/provenance_tags.re — NOT restated here (single-source invariant).

capture.description: verbatim = the original artifact is preserved as-is. reconstructed = the pattern is faithfully re-created (original in transcript/git history). Matches evals/critique_replay/cases.md's per-case label.

retrieved_at.description: When the source was captured (UTC ISO-8601 Z).

asserted_at.description: World time the case occurred / was first asserted (UTC ISO-8601 Z). Bitemporal pair with recorded_at.

supersedes_case_id.description: Append a corrected case pointing here; never edit in place (append-only, the history of belief change IS calibration data).

extension.description: Genre-specific structure (open object — documented per genre in README, deliberately not over-constrained at v1). defect-trap: {anchor, invention_traps[], gold, stratum}. capability-target: {gate, coverage_status, in_scope[], out_scope[]}. ground-truth-exemplar: usually empty.

adjudication_log.description: Append-only record of belief changes about this case (mirrors evals cases.md gold-adjudication log + the append-only-over-edit principle).
